reason: test_outputs.py has a pre-existing Python syntax error on line 414 (unterminated string literal in a heredoc node.js script) that prevents pytest from collecting the module. This is a test bug, not an instruction problem. The syntax error is in a Python string containing node.js code — the triple-quote that closes the heredoc is missing/misplaced, making the Python parser see an unterminated string literal. No instruction.md rewrite can make a broken test file pass.
